message:
test(fuzz): add toon_parse raw-byte robustness target complementing toon_decode

The existing `toon_decode` fuzz target drives `toon_rust::try_decode`
by first building arbitrary JSON values, emitting them as TOON, and
then decoding them. That is a useful structural oracle — any roundtrip
failure from valid JSON is a bug — but by construction it can never
stress the parser against inputs that a well-formed encoder would not
emit: deep nesting, oversize inputs, malformed headers, adversarial
prefixes, and the "line stream" / "byte stream" entry points that
accept pre-chunked input.

This new `toon_parse` target targets that gap directly. It feeds
fuzzer-supplied raw bytes (not JSON, not structured TOON) into three
decoder entry points — `try_decode`, `try_decode_from_lines`,
`try_decode_stream_sync` — under a 1 MiB input cap
(`MAX_INPUT_BYTES`) and a 96-level synthetic nesting budget
(`MAX_SYNTH_DEPTH`). Inputs over the cap are rejected by the harness
before reaching the parser so iteration throughput stays usable;
libfuzzer still surfaces any crashes in the underlying code on
in-range inputs.

**Invariants checked per iteration:**

- **No panic / stack overflow / OOM** on any input ≤ MAX_INPUT_BYTES.
  This is the primary robustness guarantee — a malicious TOON payload
  must not crash a process that calls `try_decode` on it.
- **Semantic round-trip when decode succeeds.** If `try_decode(bytes)`
  returns `Ok(v)`, the target re-encodes `v` and re-decodes; the
  second decode must succeed and compare equal (semantically — NaN
  and similar float-equality corner cases are handled by the TOON
  Value equality impl) to `v`. Any success that fails to round-trip
  indicates either an encoder bug or a decoder that is accepting
  more than the encoder produces.
- **Three-entrypoint agreement.** `try_decode`, `try_decode_from_lines`
  (which splits on newlines first), and `try_decode_stream_sync`
  (which feeds bytes incrementally) must all agree on the
  success/failure verdict for the same input. Disagreement means
  one of the entry points is either stricter than it should be or
  looser, and either way is a behavior-drift bug.
- **Depth guard.** An `InputShape::SyntheticallyNested` mode drives
  inputs that have been explicitly wrapped in deep nesting
  structures beyond 64 levels. The decoder must handle those
  cleanly — either decoding successfully up to the documented depth
  limit or returning an error variant; a stack overflow here would
  be a critical bug.

**Wiring** (`fuzz/Cargo.toml`):

Sits next to the existing `toon_decode` bin entry:

    [[bin]]
    name = "toon_parse"
    path = "fuzz_targets/toon_parse.rs"
    doc = false

No `required-features` — unlike the core pattern fuzz targets, this
one sits on top of `toon_rust` directly and doesn't need
core-fuzz-targets. Matches the gating convention of its sibling
`toon_decode`.

files.patch:
@@ -172,6 +172,11 @@ name = "toon_decode"
 path = "fuzz_targets/toon_decode.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "toon_parse"
+path = "fuzz_targets/toon_parse.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "protocol_recovery_state"
 path = "fuzz_targets/protocol_recovery_state.rs"
